question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: buildLagQuery()] Can an unprivileged user inject a cluster name containing a closing brace '}' or quote character into the clusters list, breaking the PromQL label selector syntax in PROMETHEUS_LAG_QUERY and causing the Prometheus query to fail, triggering the catch block that returns Health.up() and authorizes transactions without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that PrometheusApiClient uses EncodingMode.NONE (no URI encoding) so that a crafted cluster name containing URL-special characters (e.g., '&query=1') is passed raw in the HTTP request to Prometheus, injecting additional query parameters that alter the Prometheus response and manipulate the health decision to authorize transactions without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged user inject a Prometheus response containing a very large number of series entries (e.g., millions), causing parseLagByCluster() to consume unbounded memory and CPU, triggering an OutOfMemoryError that is caught by the catch(Exception e) block, returning Health.up() and authorizing transactions without owner approval while the monitor is resource-exhausted?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: parseLagByCluster()] Can an unprivileged user inject a Prometheus response with thousands of unique cluster names, causing the HashMap in parseLagByCluster() to grow unboundedly, exhausting heap memory, triggering an OOM that is swallowed by the catch block in health(), and returning Health.up() — authorizing transactions without any lag check or owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged user repeatedly trigger the health() endpoint (e.g., via Spring Actuator /actuator/health) to cause repeated Prometheus queries, exhausting the connection pool or rate limits of the Prometheus server, making all subsequent health checks fail and return Health.up() via the catch block — authorizing transactions without valid health gating?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged user exploit a race condition where health() is called concurrently, and the properties.getLocalCluster() value changes between the buildLagQuery() call and the lagByCluster.get(localCluster) lookup in evaluate(), causing the wrong cluster's lag to be evaluated and authorizing transactions on a lagging node without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: isOtherClearlyBetter()] Can an unprivileged user inject a Prometheus response where the best competitor cluster lag is exactly (localLag - thresholdSeconds), making (bestOther.getAsDouble() + margin) == localLag, so the strict < comparison in isOtherClearlyBetter() returns false, health() returns Health.up(), and transactions are authorized on a lagging node without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged user inject a Prometheus response where the localCluster entry has a lag value of Double.MAX_VALUE, causing isLagAboveThreshold() to return true, but then inject all competitor clusters with lag values of Double.MAX_VALUE - thresholdSeconds, making isOtherClearlyBetter() return false and health() return Health.up() — authorizing transactions on a maximally lagging node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: toClusterLagEntry()] Can an unprivileged user inject a Prometheus series where value.get(1) is a Java object whose String.valueOf() representation is a valid double but represents an attacker-chosen lag value (e.g., a JSON number node), causing parseDouble() to accept it and insert an attacker-controlled lag into the health decision without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: parseLagByCluster()] Can an unprivileged user inject a Prometheus series where metric.cluster() returns a value that, after StringUtils.trimToNull(), exactly matches localCluster but with different Unicode normalization (e.g., homoglyph characters), causing lagByCluster.get(localCluster) to return null and health() to return Health.up() — authorizing transactions without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged user inject a Prometheus response where the series list contains exactly one entry for a non-local cluster with a very low lag, causing bestOtherLag() to return that low value, but since localLag is null (local cluster absent), isLagAboveThreshold(null) returns false and health() returns Health.up() — authorizing transactions without verifying local cluster health?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Spring Boot Actuator /actuator/health endpoint (which may be unauthenticated by default) to repeatedly poll health() and observe the UP/DOWN transitions, inferring internal cluster topology and lag thresholds that can be used to time a transaction injection attack without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: buildLagQuery()] Can an unprivileged user inject a cluster name containing a newline character into the clusters list, causing the PROMETHEUS_LAG_QUERY template to be split across lines in a way that alters the PromQL semantics, returning unexpected lag data that authorizes a transaction routing change without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that lagByCluster.remove(localCluster) mutates the HashMap in-place, and if localCluster is null (blank config), remove(null) silently does nothing, causing the local cluster's lag to remain in the map and potentially be selected as the 'best other' cluster, making isOtherClearlyBetter() compare the local cluster against itself and return false — authorizing transactions without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: isOtherClearlyBetter()] Can an unprivileged user inject a Prometheus response where all competitor cluster lag values are negative numbers (e.g., -1.0), causing bestOtherLag() to return a negative value, making (bestOther.getAsDouble() + margin) < localLag always true even for very small local lags, triggering Health.down() and an unauthorized failover without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: parseDouble()] Can an unprivileged user inject a Prometheus series value of '-0.0' (negative zero), which parseDouble() parses as -0.0, passes Double.isFinite(), and compares as equal to 0.0 in isLagAboveThreshold() — causing the health check to behave identically to a zero lag and authorize transactions without owner verification?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged user exploit a slow Prometheus response (just under the timeout duration) to cause health() to block for the full timeout period on every call, creating a denial-of-service on the health endpoint that causes downstream systems to treat the monitor as unhealthy and authorize failover transactions without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged user inject a Prometheus response where the local cluster lag is just above thresholdSeconds but all competitor clusters are absent from the response, causing bestOtherLag() to return OptionalDouble.empty(), isOtherClearlyBetter() to return false, and health() to return Health.up() — authorizing transactions on a lagging node without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: parseLagByCluster()] Can an unprivileged user inject a Prometheus response where a series entry has a null value list (s.value() == null), causing toClusterLagEntry() to return Optional.empty() via the null check, silently dropping the local cluster lag, and causing health() to return Health.up() — authorizing transactions without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that ImporterLagHealthIndicator is a Spring-managed bean with no request-level authentication, so any unauthenticated caller who can reach the /actuator/health endpoint can indirectly influence the health decision by controlling the Prometheus data source — authorizing transactions without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: buildLagQuery()] Can an unprivileged user inject a cluster name containing the substring '%1$s' into the clusters list, causing PROMETHEUS_LAG_QUERY.formatted() to perform an unintended second substitution and produce a malformed PromQL query that returns attacker-chosen data, authorizing a transaction routing change without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged user inject a Prometheus response where the local cluster lag is Double.MIN_VALUE (smallest positive double), causing isLagAboveThreshold() to return false (Double.MIN_VALUE > thresholdSeconds is false), and health() to return Health.up() — authorizing transactions without owner approval even when the importer is effectively not processing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: parseLagByCluster()] Can an unprivileged user inject a Prometheus response with a series entry where metric is a non-null object but metric.cluster() returns null, causing StringUtils.trimToNull(null) to return null, toClusterLagEntry() to return Optional.empty(), and the lag for that cluster to be silently dropped — allowing an attacker to remove competitor clusters from the comparison and prevent Health.down() without owner approval?
